lines.content: Hey Brandon, this is Dustin with Einstein moving, how you doing?
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Oh, hey good. How's it going, Dustin?
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Doing good. Roslyn was asking me some questions and then just wanted to call and touch base with you. Chat with you a little bit. She was discussing, like our team. We're in light colored clothing and I was like, that's a unique request generally we wear the bright orange uniforms and our dark shorts. And I was asking the reasoning behind that and she had said that there's a flee issue out of the house that you're moving out of. Is that correct?
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Yeah, and, and so, it's been something that we dealt with since like February but over the past probably like two maybe like a month and a half as I've been over there we haven't seen anything over there and so.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: I you know I don't anticipate being an issue, but you know, we just want to be proactive, we don't want to have an issue at the new house. And so that, that's why I was calling you know. Making that request is just like you know, we don't want to track like a potential fleet if there is one. Cause I, even though I don't think there is one. And I think we'll be good, you know I just wanna be practice. I don't want them to like have one on them and then they both bring it into the truck and then it ends to put the other house and then we're like, you know deal, dealing with this again.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Yeah, no, I definitely share that concern. Another one of my concerns is that we service one to two moves a day on our truck and we definitely can't risk and in and in fact infestation and our trucks as well have you all had like a pest control out there to treat it.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Yeah, we've had several companies and I've also gone to like Home Depot and worked with them like getting all the right tree and stuff and so they've been out, they've been out like every week and a half doing all the things that you needed to do inside and outside and we've identified the issue to like a small section in like the, on the side of the house where like we won't even be going and so, and again like we haven't seen anything in, in a couple of months and so but, but it's been.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: So hard like dealing with that and, you know, washing everything on and on and so, and like all the rugs are gone, so it's a pretty empty house. There's you know, we haven't seen anything over there but you know, again, we just want to be, you know, we just want to be proactive about it, and so that's why I was just calling to, you know, know, really, really just like talk about it and just like, see what the options were as, and, and how flexible you all were.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Absolutely. I mean, I can't ask our team to work outside of our uniform. There would be in like, street close at that point and moving is, I don't wanna say it's like a super dirty job but you'd be surprised at the end of the day with, you know, how much kind of certain stuff, right? Well that but also like, you know, just a little dust here and there, ends up collecting all over your, you end up getting pretty filthy by the end of the day so, we would have to wear our standard uniforms. It does sound like you have the.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: The flee issue under control, you just are operating out of abundance of caution and rightfully so I've had to deal with a flee issue myself as well in the past and I had to get it treated a few times myself and then when they were finally gone, I was still super paranoid about it and sometimes I even like think I fill them on my leg still I don't know if you share that but.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Yes, exactly, and, and like, they don't like the, you know, I less me but like, my, my wife super concerned about it and like, they, they've like bit her Ank when she's like, she's like, what's happening cause it's just, you know. Anyway, we don't have to get into it. But yeah, no, I, you know, la, not not glad that you share that experience but like, there's some comments, common ground I guess.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Yeah, definitely. Yes sir and then every, everything for the most part sounds good on our end I just I do want to be super transparent and fourth come in like if, while we're moving stuff the flees end up doing being a problem we wouldn't be able to proceed with the move simply because, you know we don't want to risk and testing your new house and also our equipment and exposing our team to, to the flees as well. I certainly believe you have it under control. I just wanted to be transparent with that for you. So move, move day if something popped up.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: And, and I'm cool with that. Like if we saw something like, you know, we wouldn't want you all to be moving stuff either so like, whatever the, you know, we pay like the minimum for like the whatever the amount it would be for that day. Like I forget for like four hours or whatever but like it, in the event that happened.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Honestly, we would probably just get out of there and not even charge you anything. At that point, we wouldn't be moving anything we would just, you know. So, well, not, not quite like that. It'll, it usually ends up with like a phone call and a discussion and kinda like, just some parameters for us to go back. We've been on jobs flees are a unique one. This is honestly the first job and the five years, I've been in the office that I've heard of flees but if we're in homes and like there's cockroaches and stuff. Sometimes we have to have some difficult conversations with people but you know, we don't end up charging them anytime. When we aren't facilitating the move. We just just get out of there. So.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Cool, cool, cool, I appreciate that and, and so yeah, so I get that with the, you know but I, it, it's great that they wanted to the Oran anyway so I'm like, that's totally fine and, and really it's just more about like just informing you all and so that like, when the team's there and I'll be there to it could help like you know, check, I'll be looking around checking furniture and making sure we're all good and like we should be totally fine but, you know, just to you know, just to keep an eye out for things and make sure everything goes smoothly.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Yes sir. No, I appreciate the phone call. I appreciate you taking the time to discuss this with me. You know, I'm hoping, I'm hoping this is nothing burger and we're just, you know, sharing previous experiences and concerns from it. But, but definitely you know, if something comes up something changes, if you have any questions anything, feel free to give us a call. My name's Dustin, I'm the branch manager over here. I'm happy to chat with you and kind of work, work through all of this with you.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Okay, awesome. Appreciate it and then I don't know how your system works. Like I'm assuming like when the team gets there, they have like a job not job description but like some sort of like or their, that like some sort of notes or something that they, they're like, okay like this many rooms like you know, watch out for flee. It's like this or whatever, you know what I mean.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: So I don't really wanna put, watch out for flees on there just because you know, I don't want them, I don't want it in their heads, you know, before they get out there that there's an issue, you know, we'll definitely chat with the morning of and just kind of give them a heads-up like, you know, the situation but I don't know that I wanted it on the notes ahead of time. So they're freaking out and they almost, seriously get sick the day of the move.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Okay. No, no, no and, and I'm cool with that. I just, I wasn't sure how you wanted to handle it. But that, that's totally fine verbal works as well, that doesn't matter me.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Yes sir. Yeah I'll make sure, I'll make sure I talked to them the fifteenth is a Monday. So I'll be in here to see the team out that day and just kind of give them a brief in the morning. And you know, let them know you haven't seen anything and some time but you're still operating out of abundance caution and it should all be, should all be good to go.
lines.name: Dustin Street

lines.content: Cool, awesome. You're the best. I appreciate you giving me a call.
lines.name: Brandon Harding

lines.content: Yes sir, appreciate you have a good one. Thanks, bye-bye.
lines.name: Dustin Street
